mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls signals strong labor market conditions that support corporate earnings and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where PAYEMS growth exceeds 100k/yr despite positive signal.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the federal funds rate above 3.0% signals tightening monetary policy that increases discount rates and reduces equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.0% despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in inflation (CPIAUCSL) and decreases in interest rates (FEDFUNDS) create a 'stagflation' environment that depresses equity earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 250 and FEDFUNDS is below 1.5% despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates weakening supply-side capacity that reduces corporate profitability and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where INDPRO declines > 1.5% in 3 months despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly increase in GDP (GDPC1) reflects expanding demand that boosts corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 60% of instances where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2% in a quarter despite positive signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in residential construction (HOUST) and consumer spending (RSAFS) signals strong demand-side momentum that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where HOUST exceeds 1200k and RSAFS exceeds 480B despite positive signal.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 4.0% indicates a tight labor market that pressures wage inflation and reduces corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE falls below 4.0% despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month rise in CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 250 signals persistent price pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, depressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 250 despite negative signal.
